extraction.one_line_summary: MEEUS Jordy won the Champs-Élysées sprint after BORA-hansgrohe sacrificed SAM BENNETT's entire Tour selection to give their young sprinter the full leadout squad
tactical_patterns: BORA-hansgrohe left Bennett home entirely to resource Meeus with full leadout, revealing team willingness to sacrifice proven winners for development.
UAE sandwiched podium with Pogačar second and Yates third, demonstrating team selection strategy prioritizing depth over single leader protection.
VINGEGAARD's team lost van Aert mid-race to paternity leave yet maintained 7.5min lead, suggesting leadout strength matters less than mountain domestiques.

rider_signals.MEEUS Jordy: positive: delivered first career win on biggest stage after getting resources previously denied, validates BORA's controversial Bennett exclusion gamble
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: negative: flanked winner with two UAE riders but lost by 7:29, reveals team depth couldn't compensate for individual deficit
rider_signals.YATES Adam: positive: rode selflessly while taking third overall and stage win, shows veteran willingness to sacrifice GC ceiling for team tactics
rider_signals.CICCONE Giulio: positive: secured polka dots despite VINGEGAARD threatening takeover on Colle Loze, held 105 points suggesting early breakaway commitments matter more than late-race aggression
